rationale: The citing paper uses Anderson and Simester (2013) in its theory/background section to support a specific claim: positive advertising spillovers can be stronger when information-based switching costs are higher, highlighting a search-related moderator. This is not just a bibliography-list mention; it helps frame why spillovers and search are central to the citing paper's setup. The citing paper then positions its own contribution as differing from prior spillover work by varying targeting intensity and examining same- versus cross-genre effects on a mobile content platform.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited paper as prior empirical grounding for the idea that spillovers depend on switching costs and search conditions, and uses that logic to motivate its spillover framing. It then builds a different empirical context (targeted e-book promotions) around that foundation.
